title: nymphaeales
intro: The Nymphaeales rank is an order of flowering plants that includes aquatic and semi-aquatic species, characterized by the presence of whorled leaves and actinomorphic flowers. This order comprises approximately 70 species, including water lilies and cattails. Nymphaeales is distinct from other orders like Orchidales (which includes families like Orchidaceae), Euphorbiales (which includes families like Euphorbiaceae), and Poales (which includes families like Cyperaceae). The Nymphaeales order is a significant group of plants with unique characteristics and adaptations to aquatic environments.

families.family_desc: Nelumbonaceae is a small family of aquatic plants in the order Nymphaeales, commonly known as water lilies.
